Privacy Policy.title: Privacy Policy
Privacy Policy.1 body: ft_transcendence is an academic project built by 42 KL students. It is not a commercial service. The platform is operated locally by the team for educational and evaluation purposes only.
Privacy Policy.account body: email address, username, hashed password (bcrypt), and an optional avatar image you upload.
Privacy Policy.google body: if you choose to sign in with Google, we receive your Google account email address and display name via OAuth. We do not receive your Google password or any other Google account data beyond what is needed to create and identify your account.
Privacy Policy.profile body: optional display name and bio you provide.
Privacy Policy.gameplay body: match history (opponents, scores, dates) and tournament participation records.
Privacy Policy.chat body: messages you send in chat rooms and direct messages, stored to render conversation history.
Privacy Policy.activity body: timestamps of logins and per-day counts of games played and messages sent, used to power your activity dashboard.
Privacy Policy.session body: short-lived JSON Web Tokens stored in your browser to keep you signed in.
Privacy Policy.3 item1: We do not collect phone numbers, payment information, IP geolocation, or any third-party tracking identifiers.
Privacy Policy.3 item2: We do not use advertising cookies or third-party analytics trackers.
Privacy Policy.3 item3: We do not store any data from your Google account beyond the email address and display name provided during OAuth sign-in.
Privacy Policy.4 body: Your data is used solely to operate the platform: authenticate you, render your profile, deliver real-time messages and game events, and compute the statistics shown on your dashboard. No data is used for marketing or profiling.
Privacy Policy.5 body: We do not sell, rent, or share your data with third parties. Other registered users on the same instance can see your public profile (username, avatar, display name, match history, and any messages you send in shared rooms).
Privacy Policy.6 body: Data is stored in a SQLite database hosted alongside the application. Passwords are hashed with bcrypt and are never stored in plaintext.
Privacy Policy.7 body: You may request deletion of your account and all associated data at any time. Because this is an academic instance, deletion is performed manually upon request.
Privacy Policy.8 body: This application uses only strictly necessary cookies (session tokens). No tracking or advertising cookies are set.
Privacy Policy.9 body: This policy may be updated as the project evolves. The last updated date at the top of this page reflects the most recent revision.
Privacy Policy.10 body: For privacy-related questions, open an issue on the project repository or contact the team via 42 intra.

Terms of Service.title: Terms of Service
Terms of Service.1 body: By accessing or using ft_transcendence (the "Platform"), you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use the Platform. This is an academic project created by 42 KL students and is not a commercial service.
Terms of Service.2 item1: You must be a participant or evaluator of the 42 KL project to use this Platform.
Terms of Service.2 item2: You agree to use the Platform only for lawful purposes and in accordance with these Terms.
Terms of Service.2 item3: You may not attempt to gain unauthorized access to any part of the Platform or its infrastructure.
Terms of Service.2 item4: You may not disrupt or interfere with the Platform's availability or the experience of other users.
Terms of Service.3 body: You are responsible for maintaining the security of your account credentials. You must not share your password with others. The team reserves the right to suspend or delete accounts that violate these Terms or abuse the Platform.
Terms of Service.4 body: You are solely responsible for any content you submit to the Platform, including chat messages, display names, and avatars. You agree not to post content that is harmful, abusive, hateful, obscene, or that infringes on any third-party rights. The team reserves the right to remove any content that violates these standards.
Terms of Service.5 body: You agree to participate in games and tournaments fairly. Attempts to cheat, exploit bugs for competitive advantage, or otherwise manipulate game outcomes are prohibited and may result in account suspension.
Terms of Service.6 body: The Platform and its original content (excluding user-generated content) are the work of the 42 KL student team. The project is developed for educational purposes. The Pong game concept is a reference to the classic arcade game and is used for academic learning.
Terms of Service.7 body: The Platform is provided on an "as-is" and "as-available" basis for academic evaluation. We make no warranties, express or implied, regarding uptime, availability, or fitness for any particular purpose. The Platform may experience downtime, data loss, or other issues inherent to a development environment.
Terms of Service.8 body: To the fullest extent permitted by law, the team shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from your use of the Platform. As this is a non-commercial academic project, no financial compensation or service guarantees are implied.
Terms of Service.9 body: The team reserves the right to terminate or suspend access to the Platform at any time, for any reason, including violations of these Terms. Upon termination, your right to use the Platform will immediately cease.
Terms of Service.10 body: These Terms may be updated at any time. The last updated date at the top reflects the most recent revision. Continued use of the Platform after changes constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms.
Terms of Service.11 body: For questions about these Terms, open an issue on the project repository or contact the team via 42 intra.
